data.title: S&P 500 Top Stock Gainers Today, 5/29/26
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) advanced to a new record high on Friday, closing out May with a 4.8% gain.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Let's break down the sectors behind the performance.

Today's Top-Performing Sector 

Information technology was the top-performing sector on Friday. Dell Technologies (DELL) led the charge after reporting a blowout first-quarter earnings report, highlighted by AI server revenue growth of 757% to $16.1 billion. In addition, software stocks continued to rally higher, with NetApp (NTAP), IBM (IBM), and ServiceNow (NOW) posting strong returns.

Several information technology stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Dell Technologies (DELL)
  • NetApp (NTAP)
  • IBM (IBM)
  • ServiceNow (NOW)
  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the consumer staples sector faced the largest drawdown as investors rotated out of the defensive sector and into more speculative areas of the market. Clorox (CLX) led the group to the downside after CEO Linda Rendle announced she would step down due to health reasons and start a search for the next CEO. Costco (COST) also closed lower after reporting its fiscal third-quarter earnings.

Notable consumer staples stocks trading lower include:

  • Clorox (CLX)
  • Costco (COST)
  • Altria Group (MO)
  • Kimberly-Clark Corp (KMB)
  • Walmart (WMT)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Cash Discipline Becomes Issue, Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Surges Regardless
data.description:
Legacy automaker Ford (F) has a dilemma on its hands, and it is one that looks good for shareholders. New reports revealed that General Motors (GM) has been handing out cash like candy to its shareholders, and Ford shareholders are looking to see if it will follow suit. Ford shares blasted up nearly 6% in Friday's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

GM took a bold approach to keeping shareholder interest. Not only did it launch a $6 billion buyback back in January, it also hiked its dividend. Ford, meanwhile, engaged in some buybacks of its own, about $311 million worth. That was only a fraction of what GM rolled out. Ford also left its dividend alone. There was a clear difference here in philosophy, and some are wondering just what Ford is waiting for.

Ford may have answered that question recently with Ford Energy, the new venture that drove a lot of Ford's gains in the last week. Ford is also pouring cash into Ford Pro and the Model e program, which is still showing substantial losses. Some might think that is a prudent response to a changing market. Others might think it is unnecessarily strict. Which of these is the actual answer, meanwhile, remains to be seen.

Ford Hits Union Station

It should surprise no one that Ford is poised to celebrate America250, and is doing something just a little out of the ordinary. Union Station is a big deal, with some regarding it as "…the front door to the nation's capital." But this platform that mostly focuses on trains will instead be home to a set of vehicles and artifacts as part of a free public exhibit called Driving America Forward: A Ford Experience at Union Station.

The event will be split into seven sections, starting with the birth of Ford and how Ford got the world into driving. Another will deal with Ford on the job, how Ford vehicles help make so much of the world run. Pop culture, war, racing, and even unexpected innovations will all be part of the exhibit, simultaneously celebrating Ford and the United States at the same time.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 10 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 60.4% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.85 per share implies 20.79% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Nvidia Bets $6.5B on Photonics to Solve AI's Next Big Bottleneck
data.description:
Chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has committed at least $6.5 billion over the past three months to photonics companies, as it looks for ways to remove one of the biggest bottlenecks in AI infrastructure, according to CNBC. For context, photonics uses light to move data instead of electricity, which can make data transfer faster and more energy efficient. That matters because today's AI systems depend on massive amounts of data moving between GPUs, memory, networking chips, servers, and data centers.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

COHX: an alternative to margin or options on COHR

If those connections stay too dependent on copper and electrical signals, power usage and performance limits could become a serious obstacle to scaling AI. As a result, Nvidia has announced $2 billion investments in Lumentum (LITE), Coherent (COHR), and Marvell (MRVL) since early March, all of which are working on photonics technology. It also said it would invest $500 million in Corning (GLW) for advanced optical connectivity and joined Ayar Labs' $500 million funding round. Analysts say that this is Nvidia's way of making sure that the supply chain can scale before AI infrastructure hits a wall.

In addition, CEO Jensen Huang recently stated that Nvidia is starting to scale silicon photonics across its networking platforms and GPU-to-GPU interconnect technology, which means that the industry will need far more photonics capacity than exists today. Other major AI players are also moving in this direction, including AMD (AMD) and Microsoft (MSFT), which have backed or acquired companies tied to optical connectivity. Still, large-scale adoption will take time because photonics manufacturing is difficult.

What Is a Good Price for NVDA?

Overall, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 38 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average Nvidia price target of $308.22 per share implies 42% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Rises, FAA Suggests 777X Certification in 2027
data.description:
More good news hit for aerospace stock Boeing (BA) as the Federal Aviation Administration seems ready to start clearing new Boeing aircraft. The outlook was optimistic, and investors followed along, sending Boeing shares up modestly in Friday's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

You might remember yesterday, when Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg noted that the 737-7 was likely to be certified ahead of the 737-10. That sentiment was echoed, at least somewhat, by FAA administrator Bryan Bedford. Bedford noted that Ortberg's projections were right, saying that the MAX 7 was likely to come first, followed by the MAX 10. But Bedford took things one step farther, saying, "…hopefully the 777 early next year."

This came as a bit of a surprise, as Boeing was looking for an amended type certificate before the end of 2026. That would have opened up the potential for deliveries to start in early 2027. But if the certification does not arrive until 2027, then that likely means the delays will continue. The flight test department's workload has been "heavy," reports note, and with two other planes likely to be certified ahead of the 777X, that may explain why.

Repurposed Hardpoints

Meanwhile, reports note that Boeing is working to get more life out of the B-1B lineup by improving weapons capacity. Using a new feature called the Load Adaptable Modular multi-use weapons pylon, Boeing could effectively turn bombers in missile-carriers as well, especially long-range hypersonic missiles, reports note.

The pylon has not yet been certified, but Boeing is working on getting through the design phase that will see formerly-used hardpoints—points where weapons can be mounted—replaced with the new pylon. The pylon has been tested as far back as 2022, and tests continued into 2024, finding that the B-1B could indeed carry long-range munitions.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 15 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.35% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $274.14 per share implies 18.64% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: New FBI Warning Hits for Teams Users, Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Surges
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) has no shortage of offerings. And a new, urgent warning from the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) in the United States actually underscores just how deep Microsoft's product line is. For anyone using Teams, Outlook or OneDrive, the FBI warning is meant for you. This did not dampen investors' spirits, though, and Microsoft shares surged nearly 4% in Friday's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

A new cyberattack kit known as Kali365 has been released, the warning notes, and with it, even hackers with comparatively low skills can get access to users' accounts. With Kali365, the users can capture "Microsoft authentication tokens," which in turn can be used to bypass multifactor authentication. With the tokens, users do not even need access to a password to carry out the attack.

The kit itself offers a subscription price for hackers, and anyone willing to pay as little as $250 a month for the necessary access can put the tools therein to work. These tools include AI-generated phishing lures, dashboards to track specific entities, and OAuth token capture systems. And with hundreds of Kali365 attacks already seen, the threat is already starting to show up.

Hard Choices

Yesterday, we noted that brand awareness and support are mounting around Microsoft, and this was generally good news. Microsoft will particularly need some goodwill as it is reportedly poised to make "hard choices" about the future of Microsoft Gaming.

That line comes at a difficult time for Microsoft. It is about to walk into summer, which is basically showtime for Microsoft. Summer Game Fest, the various E3-inspired press events…this is a big time for Microsoft to show off what is coming. But we also know that video gaming in general is on something of a decline as attention gets diluted. And with chip shortages driving up prices, gaming is also much less accessible as a hobby these days. The "hard choices" could mean anything. And while current-generation gaming is safe, and the Xbox Helix is likely fine for now, gaming itself at Microsoft could be doomed down the line.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 34 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 7.25% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $556.88 per share implies 25.62% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Slips as FSD Hits Estonia
data.description:
Not so long ago, we heard how electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) does not have much of a presence in Europe, at least as far as its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) system goes. Recent reports noted it was really only available in Lithuania and the Netherlands. But now, one more country has stepped in, and Estonia will now allow FSD to operate within its borders. Tesla investors did not seem particularly pleased about this, though, and shares slipped modestly in Friday's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

Tesla, turning to its X account, revealed that Estonia had approved FSD for use in the country, and rollout would begin "soon." There was no indication of whether "soon" was measured in weeks or months, but "soon" it remained. Estonia had reportedly been testing the system on roads in the country for a year and a half, though self-driving vehicles have been operating within Estonia since 2017.

Thus, allowing Tesla's FSD to step in made a certain sense. It also allows Tesla to start gaining access to a new market of potential subscribers, which will undoubtedly be welcome. It may not be a particularly large market, but it will be a new market nonetheless, not to mention a boon to Tesla's cash flow.

Tesla Crashes Into House

In another unfortunate installment of Tesla and its odd convention of driving where it should not, a Tesla managed to hit an entire house in San Diego's Claremont neighborhood. To be fair, though, the Tesla in question apparently first collided with a vehicle before ending up crashing through a fence and a backyard canopy to ultimately hit the house in question.

Sadly, there were several injuries involved, as six people—including one child—were transported to a nearby hospital following the crash. Four of them had minor injuries, while two had what were described as "major injuries." The San Diego Police Department is currently investigating.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 27.6% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $403.86 per share implies 7.8% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Disney Stock (NYSE:DIS) Slips With Unexpected Reveal: "The Hulu Tech Stack and App Will Be Decommissioned…"
data.description:
Remember when entertainment giant Disney (DIS) said that it had "no current plans" to sunset the standalone Hulu app? Those current plans may have already changed, as a new report suggests the Hulu app has a shelf-life, and one far shorter than anyone may have expected. The potential shutdown of the Hulu app may be coming soon, and Disney shares slipped over 1.5% in Friday's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

An internal memo revealed that Disney plans to shut down the Hulu standalone app, though just when is unclear. "Eventually" was the word used in the memo, and further reports suggest it could be done by the end of 2026. The memo also noted, "The Hulu tech stack and app will be decommissioned after all users have transitioned" to Disney's operations.

Given that Hulu was not getting much in the way of new features, or any sort of development, this probably should not come as much of a surprise. It makes little sense to allow the Hulu app to stand alone when all that content can be quietly folded into Disney+, and make Disney+ that much more attractive. But still, for Hulu users, it will be a blow when Hulu is finally no more, killed by the Disney that bought it.

A Great Big Beautiful Tomorrow

Before President Trump was using "Great Big Beautiful" to describe his legislative efforts, Disney was using the term to describe tomorrow in its Carousel of Progress. Described as "the most performed stage show in the history of American theater," the show illustrates America and how technology and other developments change those who live therein. And now, it is poised for some new changes.

"Progress" pretty much implies change, after all, so this should not come as a surprise. The attraction will be adding new decades, starting with the 1960s, going into the 1980s, then the New Millennium, followed by the Possible Future. Disney will be shuttering the attraction starting July 6, 2026, with plans to reopen in 2027.

Is Disney Stock a Buy or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on DIS stock based on 17 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After an 8.24% loss in its share price over the past year, the average DIS price target of $133.59 per share implies 30.12% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: "Paying for 2030 Economics at 2026 Prices": Should Boomers Stay Out of the SpaceX IPO?
data.description:
For all those who have considered the upcoming SpaceX IPO and thought, finally, my chance to get in on the ground floor of a huge win to come, there may be reason to reconsider, depending on, of all things, your age. A new report out from The Information Briefing notes that those known as Baby Boomers may not get much out of the upcoming IPO.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

This might come as a shock, because Boomers have seen several decades of IPOs come and go. Some of them have led to staggering increases, the kind that make generational wealth happen. But The Information's Briefing noted that the SpaceX IPO makes sense for two classes of investor: day traders and younger investors. Boomers, meanwhile, could get "burned" by the process.

On the surface, that seems nuts. After all, there will be plenty of shares available to buy. Reports note that somewhere around 30% of SpaceX could be available for retail buyers. But this may mean extreme volatility, and the possibility that shares will trade near the top of valuation for a while. And there are certainly paths for SpaceX to grow into, and beyond, a hoped-for $2 trillion valuation. But Franco Granda, an analyst with Pitchbook, noted, "…investors should understand that they are paying for 2030 economics at 2026 prices."

And Then There's Tesla

Meanwhile, there are concerns that SpaceX will ultimately merge with Tesla (TSLA). This is not out of line. After all, SpaceX has regular cash flow thanks to its Starlink internet service, and Tesla needs solid cash flow right now as it makes its move from cars to robots and energy. But Wedbush's Daniel Ives—who has a five-star rating on TipRanks—suggests that such a move could be bad for Tesla shareholders in the end.

There are some resources being shared between SpaceX and Tesla. SpaceX bought a bunch of Cybertrucks not so long ago, and with the Terafab chip project and the possibility of orbital data centers coming up, Tesla and SpaceX may have more overlap than some expect. But given that Elon Musk holds about 85% control of SpaceX, folding it into Tesla may simply give Musk more control of the whole entity.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 27.6% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $403.86 per share implies 6.89% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.description:

Its AI-powered tools, such as Copilot, are now used by over 70% of Fortune 500 companies. Also, the tech giant is expanding its AI footprint globally, including a $400 million investment in Switzerland. Thus, investors looking for exposure to MSFT stock may consider investing in these two ETFs: Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT) and iShares Global Tech ETF (IXN).

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

Let's take a deeper look at these two ETFs.

Vanguard Information Technology ETF

The VGT ETF provides investors with exposure to technology stocks, including companies in software, semiconductors, internet, and other technology-related sectors. This ETF tracks the MSCI U.S. Investable Market Index/Information Technology 25/50 Index.

MSFT stock constitutes 15.11% of the ETF's holdings. Apart from MSFT, some of the top stocks in the VGT ETF are Nvidia (NVDA), Apple  (AAPL), and Broadcom (AVGO). Overall, the ETF has $94.11 billion in assets under management (AUM). Also, it has an expense ratio of 0.09%. The VGT ETF has returned 38.2% in the past three months.

Turning to Wall Street, the ETF has a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Of the 321 stocks held, 254 have a Buy, 60 have a Hold, and seven have a Sell ratings. At $725.62, the average VGT ETF price target implies a 7.66% upside potential.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Jump to New Highs as Trump Nears Iran Deal Verdict; Oil Extends Decline
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) rallied to new highs on Friday, rounding out a strong month for stocks.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Trump held a meeting in the Situation Room with top officials to provide a "final determination" on a deal with Iran. He mentioned the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the removal of mines in the waterway, and the destruction of Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium as key conditions for the U.S. to accept the deal.
  • The U.S. would lift its blockade on Iranian ports if the conditions are satisfied and begin negotiating nuclear restrictions for Iran. "No money will be exchanged, until further notice," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "Other items, of far less importance, have been agreed to."
  • Brent Crude oil (BZ) fell by over 1% and secured its largest monthly loss since 2020. Furthermore, U.S. gasoline prices declined 3.5 cents overnight, marking the largest one-day fall in 17 years.
  • Dell (DELL) skyrocketed higher after reporting that its AI server revenue surged 757% to $16.1 billion during the first quarter. The company's overall revenue accelerated by nearly 88%.
  • Software stocks continued to rise, highlighted by strong returns from ServiceNow (NOW), Salesforce (CRM), Datadog (DDOG), Palantir (PLTR), and CrowdStrike (CRWD).
  • Truist analyst Youssef Squali raised his Amazon (AMZN) price target to $320 from $310 and his Alphabet (GOOGL) target to $430 from $415.
data.author: Eddie Pan
